definition: A Russia-Ukraine settlement would be the most powerful single catalyst for returning to negative stock-bond correlation by simultaneously collapsing the energy risk premium, easing European fiscal pressure, reducing defense spending growth, and restoring central bank credibility. Market pricing (~15% settlement probability) may underweight this tail.
evidence: Settlement would address multiple positive-correlation drivers simultaneously
Energy risk premium collapse, European fiscal pressure relief, defense spending reduction
Market pricing ~15% settlement probability per risk_analyst_02 assessment
Directionally sound but geopolitical prediction is inherently low-confidence
open_questions: Is the 15% market-implied settlement probability accurate or systematically biased?
Would a partial settlement (frozen conflict) have the same correlation impact as full resolution?
How quickly would energy markets and fiscal policy adjust post-settlement?
